variable.name: Total Turnover
variable.definition: Total Turnover represents the total monetary value of all shares traded during the specified time period.
variable.methodology:
Methodology:
Turnover is calculated as:
Turnover = Sum of (Trade Price * Quantity Traded) for all transactions.

Display formats:
• Indian System: Displayed in Crores (₹ Cr) where 1 Crore = 10,000,000.
• Western System: Displayed in Billions (₹ B) or Millions (₹ M) depending on the scale.

variable.name: Traded Volume
variable.definition: Traded Volume represents the total number of shares transacted (bought and sold) during the specified time period.
variable.methodology:
Methodology:
Volume is the cumulative sum of shares traded:
Volume = Sum of (Quantity Traded) across all trades.

Display formats:
• Indian System: Displayed in Crores (Cr) or Lakhs (L) depending on the value.
• Western System: Displayed in Billions (B), Millions (M), or Thousands (K).

variable.name: Avg Close Price
variable.definition: Average Close Price is the average closing price of the stock(s), weighted by traded volume if aggregated over multiple records.
variable.methodology:
Methodology:
For a single stock on a single day, this is the official closing price (ClsPric).
For aggregated rows (e.g. across multiple companies or dates), this is the Volume-Weighted Average Price:
Weighted Price = Sum of (Close Price * Volume) / Sum of (Volume).

Display formats:
• Displayed as absolute currency in Indian Rupees (₹) rounded to 2 decimal places.

variable.name: Company Count
variable.definition: The count of unique companies or stock symbols represented within the aggregated group.
variable.methodology:
Methodology:
Calculated by counting the unique occurrences of company tickers/symbols:
Count = Size of Set(SYMBOL) within the active filter scope.
